senses.definition: Relating to or situated between islands; island-hopping or non-contiguous in a group of islands.
examples: Septinsular politics focused on cooperation among the island states.
They studied the septinsular geography of the archipelago.

senses.definition: Having seven parts or divisions.
examples: A septipartite structure was described in the architectural treatise.
The septipartite committee had seven subcommittees.

senses.definition: Having seven syllables in a line or unit of verse.
examples: The poem was septisyllabic, with seven syllables per line.
In septisyllabic meter, each line contains exactly seven syllables.

senses.definition: A unit of seven syllables in poetry or a line of verse.
examples: The line is a septisyllable, counting seven beats.
He crafted a septisyllable ending for the stanza.

senses.definition: Having seven equivalents or sevenfold value.
examples: The septivalent ratio was used in the old calculator.
A septivalent relation existed among the seven factors.

senses.definition: A historical or specialized unit of seven; a term used in some fractional or distributive systems.
examples: The ledger listed quantities in septlevas.
Septleva was discussed in the antiquarian manuscript.

senses.definition: Plural of septleva; multiple seven-based units used in certain records or systems.
examples: Accounts were measured in septlevas and fractions.
The clerk separated items into septlevas.

senses.definition: A genus of fungi that causes disease in plants.
examples: Septobasidium species can form mutualistic relationships with scale insects.
Farmers warned about septobasidium infections in their crops.

senses.definition: The septal or seven-part rib or costa in anatomy or biology.
examples: The septocosta contributes to the shell's structural integrity.
Anatomists noted the septocosta in the specimen.

senses.definition: Having seven cylindrical parts or sections.
examples: The septocylindrical widget is designed with seven chambers.
A septocylindrical core was used in the model.

senses.definition: A seven-cylindered structure or a unit with seven cylindrical parts.
examples: The engine featured a septocylindrium configuration.
Researchers studied the septocylindrium in the prototype.

senses.definition: A medical term for diarrhea involving seven related factors or episodes, used historically or in specific medical literature.
examples: The case described septodiarrhea in a case study.
Medical notes referenced septodiarrhea in the differential diagnosis.

senses.definition: A germ or microorganism associated with seven-part or septate structures; historically used in microscopy.
examples: The specimen revealed septogerm colonies.
Septogerm terminology appears in older microbiology texts.

senses.definition: A genus of fungi or fungal-like organisms; septogloeum is used in botanical or mycological contexts.
examples: Septogloeum species were observed on the bark.
Her study focused on septogloeum in tropical trees.

senses.definition: Relating to or near the septum or dividing wall; septal.
examples: The septoic region in the tissue showed inflammation.
Septoiс features were noted in the anatomical cross-section.

senses.definition: A small shelf or structure near a septum; or an anatomical term referring to a septum-related plate.
examples: The septole was attached near the wall of the chamber.
In anatomy, septole refers to a small partitioning plate.

senses.definition: A misspelling variant; treated as an alias for septole in some texts.
examples: The manuscript uses septole t inconsistently.
Cross-check the term with septole in the glossary.

senses.definition: Situated at or near the septum margin; combining septum and margin.
examples: The septomarginal region was described in the anatomical diagram.
Septomarginal fibers were analyzed in the tissue sample.

senses.definition: Relating to septum and axillary (armpit) region; anatomically describing a septum near the armpit.
examples: Septomaxillary fissures are described in some skull studies.
Theseptomaxillary features were noted in the specimen.

senses.definition: Relating to a septum and the nasal area; nasal septum related.
examples: Septonasal measurements were taken in the study.
The septal defect affected the septonasal region.

senses.definition: A genus of fungi that causes leaf spots on plants; common plant pathogen.
examples: Septoria leaf spots are common in many crops.
Agricultural researchers study septoria to protect harvests.

senses.definition: Plural of septoria; multiple species or instances of the leaf-spot fungi.
examples: The fields showed several septorias affecting crops.
Septorias were identified in the survey reports.

senses.definition: A surgical procedure involving cutting the septum or a septal structure.
examples: The patient underwent septotomy to correct the nasal septum.
Septotomy was performed under general anesthesia.

senses.definition: Abbreviation or plural form related to septa or septums; used in technical contexts.
examples: The chart lists several septs found in the specimen.
Notes refer to the septs in cross-section diagrams.

senses.definition: A ship or vessel designed with septa or dividing walls; possibly a historical term.
examples: The septship was described in maritime catalogs.
Older texts mention a peculiar septship design.
